question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R6 lowers MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R6↔R2 link, Area 0)
from 1514 to 1400 while R2's matching interface stays at 1514. Those are
the Layer-2 interface MTUs; OSPF compares the IP MTU it advertises in the
DBD Interface MTU field, which is 14 bytes lower on Ethernet — so the two
sides advertise 1386 and 1500 respectively. OSPF requires the two ends to
agree before completing database exchange, so the neighbor stalls in
EXSTART/EXCHANGE and never reaches FULL; both sides retransmit DBDs.
Iconic "ping works, OSPF doesn't" production scenario.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.106 (R6), the OSPF interface MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R6<->R2 link, area 0) was lowered to 1386 while the far end, GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 on 100.64.0.102 (R2), offers 1500.
Because the two ends of the link disagree on the interface MTU, database synchronization (the Database Description exchange) cannot complete, so the adjacency never reaches FULL and no routes are exchanged over that link.
Fix: restore the MTU on R6 GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 so both ends agree (OSPF MTU 1500; on XR, interface `mtu 1514`).
expected_symptoms_summary:
OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R6: neighbor 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "interface down or detached".

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no mtu

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:49:08.405 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: interface down or detached, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
